key_insight: A system-specific MACE model trained on SQS DFT data captures Fe-Ni structural, elastic, and thermal properties better than generic foundation models, but still misses magnetically driven phase-stability trends under high pressure.
engineering_relevance: For alloy design and multiscale simulation, targeted training on representative disordered structures can substantially improve ML potential fidelity for Fe-Ni phase behavior and thermomechanical predictions.
method: Spin-polarized PBE DFT reference data on chemically disordered SQS supercells.
Train a system-specific MACE-sqs potential across compositions, bcc/fcc phases, and volumetric/shear deformations.
Benchmark against foundation MACE models on equations of state, elastic constants, thermal expansion, and bcc-to-hcp transition pressure.
evidence_signal: MACE-sqs achieved 2.0 meV/atom validation energy error and 24.3 meV/Å force error, and showed the best overall agreement with DFT and experiment among the tested models.
watch_item: All tested models still predict the wrong composition trend for the bcc-to-hcp transition pressure, indicating unresolved magnetic-collapse and magnetoelastic effects.
paper_title: Can MACE Potentials Accurately Describe Magnetism and Phase Stability in Fe-Ni Alloys? A Systematic Benchmark
